title: Commodity Intelligence: EU Outlook
subtitle: April 2026 — key cost drivers for food & catering procurement
source: Expana (Mintec) Commodity Price Change Overview Report
trend_analysis: EU Food HICP YoY inflation peaked at +3.4% mid-2025 and has since moderated to +2.5% by January 2026. The trend suggests the post-2024 inflation wave is cooling, though underlying volatility remains high in specific categories (dairy, eggs, fresh produce) that are driving the monthly price action.

highlights.label: Extreme price risk
highlights.body: Egg US +106.2% MoM and Tomato EU +48.3% MoM / +42.0% YoY on Spanish supply shortage. Lamb UK +15.1% MoM / +22.0% YoY driven by Easter & Ramadan demand.

highlights.label: Major opportunity
highlights.body: Potato processing EU -73.9% YoY, Milk EU -73.1% YoY, Cocoa ICE London -61.5% YoY, Butter EU -42.5% YoY — window for long-term contracts.

highlights.label: Energy spillover
highlights.body: Crude oil volatility from the Middle East has pushed vegetable oils higher: Soyabean Oil US +55.5% YoY, Rapeseed Oil Canada +41.2% YoY, Palm Oil EU +4.2% YoY.
